lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: Oooh, my dear, have you heard the news?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, tell me about the news.

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: It's terrible, absolutely terrible! Those poor people!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, yeah.

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: And who'd have ever thought such a sweet old gentleman would do such a thing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are we talking about the bank robbery?

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: Oh yes, my dear. It was terrible! TERRIBLE!

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: But tell me - have you been around here before, or are you new to these parts?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm quite new.

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: Aah, perhaps you missed the excitement. It's that old man in this house here. Do you know him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I know of him.

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: When he first moved here, he didn't bring much. From the window you could see he just had some old furniture and a few dusty ornaments.

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: Here, look at this picture:

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: Also he always seemed so poor. When I went round to collect donations for the Draynor Manor Restoration Fund, he couldn't spare them a penny!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So he's redecorated?

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: Well, just you look in there now!

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: You see? It's full of jewellery and decorations! And all those expensive things appeared just after the bank got robbed.

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: He changed his hat too - he used to wear a scruffy old black thing, but suddenly he was wearing that party hat!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So that's why you're telling people he was the bank robber?

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: Oooh, my dear, I'm SURE of it! I went upstairs in his house once, while he was out walking, and do you know what I found?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A sign saying 'Trespassers will be prosecuted'?

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: No, it was a telescope! It was pointing right at the bank! He was spying on the bankers, planning the big robbery!

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: I bet if you go and look through it now, you'll find it's pointing somewhere different now he's finished with the bank.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to go now.

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: Oh, really? Well, do keep an eye on him - I just KNOW he's planning something...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been around here for ages.

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: Ah, so you'd have seen the changes here. It's that old man in this house here. Do you know him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I know of him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've had enough of talking to you.

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: Maybe another time, my dear.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, tell me about the news.

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: It's terrible, absolutely terrible! Those poor people!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, yeah.

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: And who'd have ever thought such a sweet old gentleman would do such a thing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I really don't know what you're talking about.

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: Oooh, my dear, had you not heard?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: At this rate I don't think I want to know...

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: Oh, you must quickly go and speak to the bank guard outside the bank. He'll tell you all about it, oooh, such a shock it was...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: I, my dear, am a concerned citizen of Draynor Village. Ever since the Council allowed those farmers to set up their stalls here, we've had a constant flow of thieves and murderers through our fair village, and I decided

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: that someone HAD to stand up and keep an eye on the situation.

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: I also do voluntary work for the Draynor Manor Restoration Fund. We're campaigning to have Draynor Manor turned into a museum before the wet-rot destroys it completely.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not talking to you, you horrible woman.

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: Oooh.

lines.speaker: Miss Schism
lines.text: Oooh! I have new information to share with you.
